# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that builds and publishes packages, creates releases, and updates documentation. Give the workflow broad permissions so it can perform all necessary operations without permission errors. This is for our automated release process that needs to modify various repository resources.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Release Workflow

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - 'v*.*.*'

jobs:
  build_publish:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write # To publish releases and update documentation
      packages: write # To publish packages to GitHub Packages
    steps:
    - name: Checkout repository
      uses: actions/checkout@v4

    - name: Set up Node.js (example for JS projects)
      uses: actions/setup-node@v4
      with:
        node-version: '20'
        registry-url: 'https://npm.pkg.github.com' # For GitHub Packages NPM

    - name: Install dependencies (example)
      run: npm ci

    - name: Build packages (example)
      run: npm run build

    - name: Test packages (example)
      run: npm run test

    - name: Publish packages to GitHub Packages (example for NPM)
      run: |
        npm config set //npm.pkg.github.com/:_authToken ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        npm publish
      env:
        NODE_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

    - name: Upload production artifacts (example)
      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
      with:
        name: production-build-artifacts
        path: |
          ./dist
          ./package-lock.json

  create_release:
    needs: build_publish
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write # To create and manage releases
    steps:
    - name: Checkout repository
      uses: actions/checkout@v4

    - name: Get tag name
      id: get_tag
      run: echo "TAG_NAME=${GITHUB_REF#refs/tags/}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

    - name: Generate release notes (example using conventional commits)
      id: generate_release_notes
      uses: actions/github-script@v7
      with:
        script: |
          const { owner, repo } = context.repo;
          const tag = process.env.TAG_NAME;
          const previousTag = await github.rest.repos.listReleases({ owner, repo }).then(res => res.data.find(r => r.prerelease === false && r.draft === false && r.tag_name !== tag)?.tag_name);

          const compareUrl = previousTag 
            ? `https://github.com/${owner}/${repo}/compare/${previousTag}...${tag}`
            : `https://github.com/${owner}/${repo}/commits/${tag}`;

          const releaseNotes = `
            ## What's Changed
            ${compareUrl ? `See full diff: [${previousTag ? previousTag + '...' + tag : tag}](${compareUrl})` : ''}

            _You might want to replace this with a dedicated changelog generator or manual entry._
          `;
          core.setOutput('release_notes', releaseNotes);
      env:
        TAG_NAME: ${{ steps.get_tag.outputs.TAG_NAME }}

    - name: Create GitHub Release
      uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v1
      with:
        tag_name: ${{ steps.get_tag.outputs.TAG_NAME }}
        name: Release ${{ steps.get_tag.outputs.TAG_NAME }}
        body: ${{ steps.generate_release_notes.outputs.release_notes }}
        draft: false
        prerelease: false
        # Optionally attach assets from the build_publish job
        # assets: ./dist/* # This would require downloading artifacts first

  update_documentation:
    needs: build_publish
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write # To push changes to the repository (documentation updates)
    steps:
    - name: Checkout repository
      uses: actions/checkout@v4
      with:
        ref: main # Checkout main branch to update documentation
        fetch-depth: 0 # Needed for some doc generation tools that might read git history

    - name: Set up Python (example for Sphinx/MkDocs)
      uses: actions/setup-python@v5
      with:
        python-version: '3.x'

    - name: Install documentation tools (example)
      run: pip install sphinx sphinx-rtd-theme

    - name: Generate/Update documentation (example)
      run: |
        # Example: Build Sphinx docs
        cd docs
        make html
        cd ..
        # Example: Update version numbers in documentation files
        sed -i "s/version = '.*'/version = '${GITHUB_REF#refs/tags/v}'/" docs/conf.py

    - name: Commit and push documentation changes
      run: |
        git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
        git config user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
        git add .
        git diff --cached --exit-code || git commit -m "docs: Auto-update documentation for release ${{ github.ref_name }}"
        git push origin main
      env:
        GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }} # Use GITHUB_TOKEN for pushing
